mission.title: kepler: [Tracking] Kepler Adopters
mission.description: [Tracking] Kepler Adopters. This issue affects 3+ users.

steps.title: Identify kepler feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kepler deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kepler
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: [Tracking] Kepler Adopters

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kepler resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
### Which document would you like to be mentioned?

This is a proposal to add a process for tracking Kepler adopters.

Once there is enough interest in this issue, we could move to a formal process fo

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Tracking] Kepler Adopters
steps.description:
Fixes [kepler#845](https://github.com/sustainable-computing-io/kepler/issues/845)

Gomplate is used to loop through the data/adopters.yaml file and render this list using the templates/adopters.md template. 
Therefore, future additions can be done by users by adding to data/adopters.yaml and raising PRs. This process is also described in the contributing docs.
I also wrote how that should be done through GitHub Actions in theory but could not test it yet.

Would be lovely to add GitHub Actions f

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm [Tracking] Kepler Adopters is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Fixes [kepler#845](https://github.com/sustainable-computing-io/kepler/issues/845)

Gomplate is used to loop through the data/adopters.yaml file and render this list using the templates/adopters.md template. 
Therefore, future additions can be done by users by adding to data/adopters.yaml and raising PRs. This process is also described in the contributing docs.
I also wrote how that should be done . This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kepler installed or the issue environment reproducible.
